preview.tournamentOverview: Aronimink Golf Club hosts the PGA Championship with a layout that places a premium on approach play and total strokes gained. The course rewards players who can hit greens from distance and avoid big numbers rather than relying on putting. This major carries extra weight as a test of ball-striking consistency under PGA-level pressure.
preview.modelExplainer: SG Approach receives the highest weight at 30.7 percent, followed by SG Total at 22.8 percent and SG Off-the-Tee at 16.2 percent. These three categories dominate because the model identifies iron play and overall scoring as the clearest edges at Aronimink. Driving accuracy and putting carry lower weights of 4.2 percent and 8.3 percent, confirming the course profile favors ball-strikers over short-game specialists.
preview.pickApproach: The model ranks players by blending current form with course-specific weights to produce a tournamentRank. Top-tier selections focus on golfers whose SG Approach and SG Total numbers align with the heavy course emphasis. Mid-tier bets target value where powerRank exceeds tournamentRank, while fade candidates are those whose skill sets fall outside the weighted profile.
